reformer.name: Muhammad Aurangzeb (Finance Minister) under PM Shehbaz Sharif
reformer.role: Finance Minister since 11 March 2024; ex-JPMorgan/Habib Bank CEO technocrat; PM Shehbaz Sharif (PML-N-led coalition) re-elected 3 March 2024
reformer.nextElection: By 28 February 2029 (National Assembly elected 8 Feb 2024, 5-year term)
reformer.notes: Orthodox IMF-anchored technocrat program that is over-delivering on fiscal: 9M-FY26 deficit 0.7% of GDP (lowest ever), primary surplus 3.2%, three rating upgrades in 2025, Eurobond market re-entry Apr 2026, FBR/AI tax reform, privatization of DISCOs/airports announced. Still in office as of 7 Jul 2026 (active post-budget/FY28 consultations). Not Milei-grade: no popular mandate for shock therapy - reform rests on a contested 2024 election, jailed opposition leader Imran Khan, and army backing (27th constitutional amendment Nov 2025 expanded army chief powers and immunity, sparked PTI/TTAP nationwide protests). Fuel-price street anger since the US-Israel-Iran war is the main near-term political threat; election itself is far off (2029). Score rationale: orthodox and executing well (above 50) but constrained by hybrid civil-military regime and lack of electoral mandate (well below shock-therapy grade).

parallelFx.notes: As of 7 Jul 2026: interbank USD/PKR ~279.30, regulated open-market ~279.20 (forex.pk/PakBiz data) - effectively zero gap; the interbank/open-market split was closed by the Sept 2023 ISI/FIA crackdown on hawala/hundi networks and the gap has stayed near zero through the 2026 oil shock. Caveat: an UNREGULATED grey/hawala tier persists; mid-2025 reporting put grey rates around Rs295-300 (~5% over interbank), but no verified July-2026 grey-market quote was found - treat the ~5% hawala premium as stale/unverified. Currency is a managed float under the IMF EFF, not a hard peg; no step-devaluation in the last 12 months (PKR ~279 vs ~278 a year ago).

countryRisk.rating: Moody's Caa1 stable (upgraded from Caa2, 13 Aug 2025); S&P B- stable (upgraded from CCC+, 24 Jul 2025); Fitch B- stable (upgraded, Apr 2025)
countryRisk.notes: 320bps is the spread over comparable USTs achieved on the new $500m 3-year Eurobond issued 17 Apr 2026 (~7% yield) - Pakistan's first international bond since 2022; a current secondary-market EMBI/T-spread level could not be verified (Cbonds index page blocked, worldgovernmentbonds data failed to load), so treat 320bps as the April 2026 primary-market print for the short end, with longer tenors likely wider. Trend is clearly falling: from market shut-out and near-default pricing in 2022-23 to three agency upgrades in 2025 and restored market access at single-digit yields in 2026. No evidence of spread blowout after the Middle East war oil shock, though analysts note the real test is extending the curve beyond 5 years.

PAK.imfProgram: Active and on track. 37-month EFF of ~$7.0bn plus 28-month RSF of ~$1.4bn (RSF approved 9 May 2025) - combined ~$8.4bn. IMF Executive Board completed the THIRD EFF review and SECOND RSF review on 8 May 2026, releasing ~$1.1bn (EFF) + ~$220m (RSF), i.e. ~$1.32bn, bringing cumulative disbursements to ~$4.8bn. Staff-level agreement for that review was reached 27 Mar 2026. IMF cited strong fiscal performance (FY26 primary surplus 1.6% of GDP expected, in line with targets) and reserves rebuilding (gross reserves $16bn end-Dec 2025 vs $14.5bn end-Jun 2025). IMF rejected the government's April 2026 request for larger fuel subsidies during the oil-price shock.

latestInflation.notes: CPI +11.1% YoY in June 2026 (urban 11.2%, rural 10.9%), -0.3% m/m. Down from 11.7% in May 2026 but massively re-accelerated from 3.2% in June 2025 - far above SBP's 5-7% target. Driver is exogenous: US-Israel-Iran war pushed petrol ~48% and diesel ~38% above pre-war levels (transport CPI +25.7% YoY); food re-accelerated to 9.4% (highest since Apr 2024) partly on 2025 flood damage. SBP hiked 100bps to 11.5% in Apr 2026 (first hike since Jun 2023) and held on 15 Jun 2026, expecting double-digit inflation to persist in coming months. Trend marked 'rising' on the 12-month trajectory (3.2%->11.1%) despite the one-month easing in June.

PAK.fiscalNote: Strongly improving - the standout leg of the thesis. 9M-FY26 (Jul 2025-Mar 2026): overall fiscal deficit 0.7% of GDP (vs 2.6% a year earlier), first time in Pakistan's history below 1% for the period; primary surplus 3.2% of GDP (vs 3.0% 9M-FY25). Full-year FY26: primary surplus of 1.6% of GDP expected, in line with IMF targets; Moody's (Aug 2025) expected the FY26 overall deficit to narrow to 4.5-5% of GDP from 5.4% in FY25, and Business Recorder reported FY26 tracking toward a 21-year-low deficit. FY2026-27 budget approved by parliament 23 Jun 2026: Rs18.77tn ($67.5bn) outlay, deficit target 3.6% of GDP, growth 4%, inflation 8.2% assumed - but defense spending up ~18%, and war-driven fuel-subsidy pressure is the key slippage risk.
developments: IMF Executive Board completed the 3rd EFF / 2nd RSF reviews on 8 May 2026, releasing ~$1.32bn and bringing total disbursements to ~$4.8bn of the ~$8.4bn combined program [source: https://www.imf.org/en/news/articles/2026/05/08/pr-26147-pakistan-imf-completes-3rd-rev-of-extended-arrangement-under-eff-and-2nd-rev-arrang-rsf]

Pakistan returned to international capital markets after 4 years on 17 Apr 2026 with a $500m 3-year Eurobond at ~7% (~320bps over USTs), with strong demand [source: https://www.arabnews.com/node/2640263/pakistan]

US-Israel-Iran war oil shock: monthly oil import bill jumped from ~$300m to ~$800m; petrol ~48% and diesel ~38% above pre-war levels; PM Sharif said it 'erased' two years of economic progress; IMF rejected Pakistan's April request for bigger fuel subsidies [source: https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2026/4/30/soaring-fuel-prices-in-pakistan-threaten-economic-and-political-crises]

Inflation re-accelerated to double digits: 11.7% YoY in May and 11.1% in June 2026 (vs 3.2% in June 2025), far above SBP's 5-7% target; food inflation at 9.4%, highest since Apr 2024 [source: https://www.brecorder.com/news/40428026/pakistan-inflation-hits-111-in-june-2026]

SBP hiked the policy rate 100bps to 11.5% in April 2026 - the first hike since June 2023 - and held at 11.5% on 15 June 2026, citing war-driven fuel prices and fiscal-slippage risk; SBP FX reserves $17.2bn as of 5 Jun 2026 [source: https://tradingeconomics.com/pakistan/interest-rate]

Historic fiscal outturn: 9M-FY26 deficit of 0.7% of GDP (first time ever below 1%) with a 3.2% of GDP primary surplus; IMF cleared ~$1.32bn shortly after [source: https://www.arabnews.com/node/2643302/pakistan]

Parliament approved the Rs18.77tn ($67.5bn) FY2026-27 budget on 23 June 2026: deficit target 3.6% of GDP, 4% growth and 8.2% inflation assumptions, ~18% defense spending increase, export tax abolition, AI-based FBR tax administration, next-phase privatization of DISCOs/GENCOs/airports [source: https://www.arabnews.com/node/2648250/pakistan]

FY26 GDP growth came in at 3.7% - fastest in four years though below target - with record remittances of $41-42bn and FM Aurangzeb guiding FX reserves to ~$18.4bn at FY26 close [source: https://www.dawn.com/news/2006877]
killCriteriaWatch: Inflation leg of the thesis has partially REVERSED (closest to tripping): CPI went from 3.2% YoY (Jun 2025) to 11.1% (Jun 2026) on the war oil shock, forcing SBP's first rate hike since 2023 - exogenous rather than policy failure, but 'crushed inflation' no longer holds; watch whether it decelerates toward the budget's 8.2% FY27 assumption

Fiscal slippage risk building but not tripped: IMF-rejected fuel-subsidy demands, ~18% defense spending increase, and 2025 flood costs all pressure the FY27 3.6% deficit target; the primary surplus (3.2% of GDP 9M-FY26) still provides a large buffer

Reformer/political risk elevated but not tripped: fuel-price street anger (JUI-F, opposition protests), jailed Imran Khan and PTI mobilization against the Nov 2025 27th amendment; the reform program's survival depends on army backing rather than a popular mandate - durable until the 2029 election but brittle to unrest

Parallel-gap reopening: not tripped - interbank and open-market rates are at par (~279) as of 7 Jul 2026 - but the unregulated hawala tier showed a ~5% premium in mid-2025 reporting and a sustained $800m/month oil import bill is the trigger to watch
Country-risk blowout: NOT close to tripping - three upgrades in 2025, market re-entry at ~320bps in Apr 2026, no evidence of post-shock spread widening in sources found

reformer.name: Mehmet Simsek (Treasury & Finance Minister), with CBRT Governor Fatih Karahan
reformer.role: Minister of Treasury and Finance (since June 2023); Karahan runs monetary policy
reformer.nextElection: Presidential + parliamentary due by May 2028; early-election speculation ongoing (a presidential advisor floated April 16, 2028) as a legal route to let Erdogan run a third term; Simsek says probability of early elections is 'very low' (June 25, 2026)
reformer.notes: Orthodox but constrained. Simsek's mid-2023 program (tight money, fiscal discipline, 3-stage roadmap now in 'final/structural transformation' phase) is genuine and IMF-endorsed: inflation down from 75% (May 2024) to 32.1% (June 2026), Moody's/Fitch/S&P all upgraded or raised outlooks since mid-2025. But he has no mandate of his own - the program exists at Erdogan's pleasure. 2026 shocks forced retreat: CBRT raised its 2026 inflation target from 16% to 24% (May 14, 2026), fiscal slipped in May 2026, and Erdogan's judicial crackdown on the opposition (Imamoglu jailed since Mar 2025; CHP leadership annulled by court May 22, 2026) repeatedly hits markets the program must then stabilize. Not Milei-grade: no free float (managed crawl defended with ~$55bn of reserves in March 2026), no fiscal surplus, official CPI disputed (ENAG estimates 51.4% vs official 32.1%).

parallelFx.notes: No meaningful parallel/black-market rate: the lira is convertible and trades onshore/offshore at a single rate under a heavily managed 'controlled crawl' float. USDTRY ~46.84 on 2026-07-07 (Trading Economics); record low past 45.5 in mid-May 2026 after CBRT flagged slower disinflation; lira -17% y/y (less than inflation, i.e., real appreciation continues). No hard capital controls, but heavy state management: CBRT sold/swapped tens of billions of USD (total reserves down ~$55bn in about a month after the Feb 28, 2026 Iran war shock; ~120 tons of gold in two weeks per Reuters) and intervened again after the May 22 CHP court ruling; macroprudential measures support lira deposits. A dual-rate episode was last a risk in 2023, not currently. Parallel rate field null because none is quoted.

countryRisk.rating: Moody's Ba3 stable (upgraded from B1 on Jul 25, 2025); Fitch BB- with POSITIVE outlook (outlook raised Jan 23, 2026); S&P BB-/B stable (affirmed Apr 17, 2026). All three notches below investment grade
countryRisk.notes: Spread figure is 5Y USD CDS ~242bps as of week 24 (mid-June) 2026 per MacroMicro; EMBI not directly findable. Trend: modestly rising from the best levels - CDS entered 2026 below 210bps (and touched ~232bps, lowest since 2018, per Daily Sabah) before the Feb 28 Iran-war shock ($12bn carry-trade outflow in two weeks, oil near $100) and the May 22 CHP court ruling widened it. Not a blowout: ratings momentum still positive (Fitch positive outlook; Moody's next review Jul 24, 2026). 10Y local bond yield 30.8% (Jun 26, 2026).

TUR.imfProgram: none - no IMF financing arrangement. Regular surveillance only: IMF Executive Board concluded the 2025 Article IV consultation on Feb 13, 2026, praising disinflation (49.4% Sep 2024 to 30.9% Dec 2025), strong fiscal consolidation and tight monetary stance; growth seen at 4.2% in 2026

latestInflation.notes: Official TurkStat: 32.11% y/y (vs 32.61% May), lowest since March 2026; m/m +0.99% (vs +1.71% May), smallest gain in six months. Path was non-monotonic in 2026: ~30.9% Dec 2025, re-accelerated to ~32.4% by April on the Middle East war energy shock, now easing again. CBRT year-end 2026 forecast 26% (revised up from 15-21% range); interim target raised from 16% to 24% on May 14, 2026. Policy rate held at 37% at the June 2026 MPC (third straight hold; corridor 35.5-40%), cuts paused since March, hikes 'on the table'. Credibility caveat: independent ENAG group estimates 51.4% y/y for June - a ~19pt gap vs official.

TUR.fiscalNote: Deficit, worsening at the margin. 2025 consolidated deficit ~3.1% of GDP (Dec 2025, CEIC); 2024 was 4.9%. 2026 MTP (in force since Sep 7, 2025) targets 3.5% of GDP deficit, which Simsek insists is 'well within reach'. But May 2026 swung to a TRY 298.2bn ($6.4bn) deficit vs a TRY 235.2bn surplus a year earlier - tax receipts -22.1% y/y, income tax -57%, primary balance flipped to TRY 169.3bn deficit; Jan-May spending +37.4% y/y vs revenues +33.9%; April deficit nearly doubled y/y. No surplus in sight - the 'kill the deficit' leg of the thesis is absent; this is gradual consolidation, currently slipping.
developments: Feb 28, 2026: outbreak of the US-Israel-Iran war triggered a regional shock - ~$12bn of carry-trade outflows from Turkey in the first two weeks of March (position had topped $60bn in late January), oil near $100/bbl, CBRT forced to defend the lira [source: https://www.agbi.com/analysis/markets/2026/04/turkey-back-on-carry-trade-radar/]

Mar 2026: CBRT sold/swapped tens of billions in FX and gold to hold the lira - total reserves fell ~$55bn in about a month (gross reserves had hit $208bn on Feb 6) and gold reserves fell ~120 tons in two weeks; policymakers raised the interbank overnight rate to 40% and investors saw outright hikes as possible [source: https://www.investing.com/news/economy-news/turkish-policymakers-defend-steps-investors-see-rate-hike-possible-4597605]

May 14, 2026: CBRT capitulated on targets - 2026 inflation target raised from 16% to 24% (2027: 9% to 15%), year-end forecast to 26%, citing 'extraordinary geopolitical developments'; Karahan kept 'all policy options on the table' including hikes if reserve pressure resumes [source: https://www.centralbanking.com/central-banks/monetary-policy/7975894/turkey-raises-inflation-target-to-24-citing-war-risks]

May 22, 2026: Ankara appeals court annulled the CHP's 2023 congress, ousting opposition leader Ozgur Ozel and reinstating Kilicdaroglu as interim leader - CHP calls it 'a coup through the judiciary'; markets reeled and the central bank intervened in FX to contain the lira [source: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/5/22/turkish-court-ousts-leader-of-main-opposition-party]

Jul 6, 2026: jailed Istanbul mayor Ekrem Imamoglu - Erdogan's main rival and CHP's 2028 presidential candidate, in pretrial detention since March 2025 - faced three court hearings in one day (corruption + espionage cases; prosecutors seek up to 2,352 years); 16+ elected CHP mayors arrested since 2024 [source: https://turkishminute.com/2026/07/06/jailed-erdogan-rival-faces-3-hearings-in-one-day-as-nato-leaders-arrive-in-ankara/]

Jul 3, 2026: June CPI printed 32.1% y/y (from 32.6%), lowest since March, m/m +0.99% smallest in six months - disinflation resumed after the spring energy shock, but independent ENAG estimate is 51.4% [source: https://www.rte.ie/news/business/2026/0703/1581559-turkey-inflation-rate/]

Jun 2026: May budget swung to a TRY 298.2bn ($6.4bn) deficit from a year-ago surplus as tax receipts plunged 22% y/y - opposition warns of 'fiscal collapse'; 3.5%-of-GDP 2026 deficit target under strain [source: https://www.turkiyetoday.com/business/turkiyes-budget-turns-to-64b-deficit-in-may-as-tax-receipts-plunge-3221981]

Ratings momentum intact despite shocks: Fitch raised Turkey's outlook to positive at BB- (Jan 23, 2026), S&P affirmed BB-/B stable citing continued policy discipline (Apr 17, 2026), Moody's Ba3 stable since its Jul 25, 2025 upgrade with next review Jul 24, 2026 [source: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-01-23/fitch-upgrades-turkey-s-rating-outlook-moody-s-stays-put]
killCriteriaWatch: Forced devaluation / FX pressure - CLOSEST TO TRIPPING: lira hit record lows (past 45.5 mid-May, 46.8 on Jul 7); CBRT burned ~$55bn of reserves in roughly a month (Mar 2026) and intervened again after the May 22 CHP ruling; the 'float' is a defended crawl. No parallel gap yet, but the defense is expensive and geopolitics-dependent.

Reformer losing power - MEDIUM and rising: Simsek and Karahan remain in place with Erdogan's backing and Simsek calls early-election risk 'very low', but the program is hostage to Erdogan's political project - opposition crackdown (Imamoglu jailed, CHP leadership annulled) keeps shocking markets, and third-term/early-election maneuvering (April 2028 floated) raises odds of a populist spending pivot into any election.

Budget flipping back to deficit - PARTIALLY TRIPPED in spirit: Turkey never reached surplus (thesis leg absent); trajectory worsened in Apr-May 2026 (May: TRY 298bn deficit vs year-ago surplus, tax receipts -22% y/y, primary balance negative). Watch whether the 3.5%-of-GDP 2026 target survives.

Country risk blowing out - NOT TRIPPED: 5Y CDS ~242bps vs ~210-232 at the start of 2026 - modest widening, not a blowout; all three agencies held or improved ratings through the shocks. Watch Moody's Jul 24, 2026 review.

Disinflation stalling (thesis-adjacent): CBRT already raised its 2026 target 16%->24% and ended the cutting cycle; official CPI stuck above 30% since Dec 2021 and ENAG's 51.4% estimate questions the official series.

reformer.name: Volodymyr Zelensky (President), with PM Yulia Svyrydenko and Finance Minister Serhii Marchenko running the IMF/EU-anchored economic program
reformer.role: President; PM Svyrydenko and FinMin Marchenko lead fiscal/reform execution; NBU runs orthodox monetary policy
reformer.nextElection: None scheduled - presidential/parliamentary elections barred under martial law (extended for the 19th time until 2 Aug 2026). Zelensky says elections only after a ceasefire and security guarantees; in Dec 2025 he floated legislation to allow wartime elections ~60-90 days after security guarantees. Only 10% of Ukrainians want elections before a ceasefire (Jan 2026 poll).
reformer.notes: This is orthodox technocratic management under an IMF anchor, not Milei-style shock therapy: NBU keeps a tight 15% policy rate and near-unified managed-float FX, and the new 48-month EFF (approved Feb 2026) carries revenue-mobilization and governance conditionality (VAT parcel exemption removal, transfer-pricing, simplified-tax-regime reform). But there is no fiscal consolidation - the 2026 budget plans an 18.4%-of-GDP war deficit financed by foreign aid - and reform credibility took a major hit from the Nov 2025 Energoatom corruption scandal ($110M scheme exposed by NABU; top ministers resigned; Atlantic Council called it the biggest scandal of Zelensky's presidency). Score 40 = credible orthodox team, IMF/EU anchor, but war-constrained, no electoral mandate test, and anticorruption backsliding risk.

parallelFx.notes: No classic black market - the hryvnia moved from a hard peg to NBU 'managed flexibility' in Oct 2023 and the cash market is legal - but martial-law FX controls remain and a small cash-vs-official gap re-emerged in June 2026. On 30 Jun 2026 the official rate was 44.85 UAH/USD while bank/exchange-office cash selling rates were 45.05–45.30 (I use the 45.18 midpoint; gap ~0.4–1.0%). Official rate on 7 Jul 2026: 44.4967 (TradingEconomics). NBU declared a 2026 corridor of UAH 45/USD ±5% (42.75–47.25) and is defending it with heavy intervention: $4.63bn of FX sold in June vs $3.03bn in May; analysts (KYT Group) see ~46.5/USD by end-2026.

countryRisk.rating: S&P CCC+ FC/stable (upgraded from SD on 22 Jan 2026 after GDP-warrant restructuring); Fitch CCC FC (upgraded from Restricted Default ~22 Dec 2025 after 99% of GDP-warrant holders accepted exchange; LC affirmed CCC+); Moody's Ca/stable (affirmed 30 May 2025)
countryRisk.notes: Could not verify a current EMBI/T-spread bps print (Cbonds and worldgovernmentbonds pages blocked/not loading), so spread_bps is null. Price evidence: restructured USD bonds still trade at deep-distress levels but at/near post-restructuring highs - the 2034 bond rose ~4c to ~62 cents in Apr 2026 on peace-talk optimism (implying spreads still in the thousands of bps); bonds fell up to 1.9c when Geneva talks resumed Feb 2026 and remain a headline-driven peace trade. Trend since the Aug 2024 debt + Jan 2026 warrant restructurings is improving (two upgrades in ~13 months), but risk would blow out on a definitive peace-talks collapse.

UKR.imfProgram: Active. New 48-month Extended Fund Facility of ~US$8.1bn approved by the IMF Executive Board on 26 Feb 2026 (SLA 26 Nov 2025; replaced/cancelled the 2023 EFF whose 8th review completed Jun 2025). Staff-level agreement on the FIRST review plus 2026 Article IV reached 12 Jun 2026: ~US$690M (SDR 503M) upon Board approval, bringing total disbursements under the new program to ~US$2.2bn. IMF says program remains fully financed on continued large-scale external support; 2026 GDP growth projected at only 1.0-1.6%.

latestInflation.notes: May 2026 CPI: 8.2% YoY (down from 8.6% in Apr), +0.9% m/m (minfin.com.ua index 100.9); core 7.9% YoY. June 2026 print not yet published by Derzhstat as of 7 Jul 2026. Disinflation continuing but above NBU forecast - fuel +38.7% YoY (Middle East war spillover) keeps transport inflation ~20%. NBU held its key rate at 15% on 18 Jun 2026 (third straight hold after a 50bp cut from 15.5% in Jan 2026) and signaled readiness to hike if energy costs feed core inflation.

UKR.fiscalNote: Massive structural war deficit - this thesis leg is absent. The 2026 budget plans a deficit of ~UAH 1.9trn, 18.4% of GDP (~$41.5bn per Reuters; ~$45bn per OSW at different FX), with defense/security spending alone at UAH 2.8trn (~27.2% of GDP). Revenues ~UAH 2.9trn vs expenditures ~UAH 4.8trn. Ministry of Finance estimated a €16bn unfunded gap for 2026 and a $60.8bn external financing need for 2026-27 (IMF estimate ~$63bn); OSW judges the budget "unrealistic" (defense underfunded, civilian spending 100% dependent on foreign money). Financing was largely secured by the 19 Dec 2025 EU deal (€90bn/$105bn for 2026-27 via joint EU borrowing). IMF (Jun 2026) says expenditures will stay elevated over the medium term; program pushes revenue mobilization (VAT parcel exemption removal, transfer-pricing, simplified-tax-regime curbs). Trajectory: deficits of this scale persist as long as the war does.
developments: IMF Executive Board approved a new ~US$8.1bn 48-month EFF for Ukraine on 26 Feb 2026, replacing the 2023 arrangement [source: https://www.imf.org/en/news/articles/2026/02/26/pr-26066-ukraine-imf-executive-board-approves-usd-8point1-billion-under-an-eff-arrangement]

IMF staff-level agreement on the first EFF review + 2026 Article IV reached 12 Jun 2026: ~$690M access (total ~$2.2bn disbursed); 2026 growth cut to 1.0-1.6% on war and Middle East spillovers [source: https://www.imf.org/en/news/articles/2026/06/12/pr-26202-ukraine-imf-reaches-agreement-1st-rev-of-eff-arrang-2026-art-iv-consult]

Fitch upgraded Ukraine to CCC from Restricted Default (~22 Dec 2025) after 99% of investors accepted the GDP-warrant exchange, completing restructuring of 94% of commercial external debt [source: https://www.investing.com/news/economy-news/ukraines-credit-rating-upgraded-by-fitch-ratings-93CH-4420332]

EU leaders agreed on 19 Dec 2025 to a €90bn ($105bn) interest-free loan for Ukraine's 2026-27 needs via joint EU borrowing, after Belgium blocked the frozen-Russian-assets 'reparations loan' [source: https://www.npr.org/2025/12/19/g-s1-102967/eu-leaders-agree-on-ukraine-loan]

US-brokered peace process missed its June 2026 deadline: Geneva/Abu Dhabi rounds produced no breakthrough over Donbas withdrawal demands; UK/France/Germany backed direct dialogue on 7 Jun 2026; Ukraine's 2034 dollar bond hit ~62 cents in April on truce hopes [source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_United_States%E2%80%93Ukraine%E2%80%93Russia_meetings_in_Geneva]

NABU exposed an alleged $110M corruption scheme at state nuclear firm Energoatom on 10 Nov 2025; top ministers resigned and PM Svyrydenko ordered an energy/defense-sector audit - the biggest graft scandal of Zelensky's presidency, rattling Western backers [source: https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/blogs/ukrainealert/zelenskyy-faces-the-biggest-corruption-scandal-of-his-presidency/]

War intensity undiminished: massive Russian missile/drone strike on Kyiv killed at least 22 on 2 Jul 2026; NBU sold $4.63bn of FX in June (vs $3.03bn in May) to defend the hryvnia as the cash-market gap widened [source: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/2/kyiv-attacked-after-ukraines-zelenskyy-warns-of-massive-russian-strike]
killCriteriaWatch: Budget flipping back to deficit: ALREADY FAILED for this thesis - Ukraine never had a surplus; 2026 plans an 18.4%-of-GDP deficit wholly dependent on foreign financing, and OSW judges even that understated

Forced devaluation / parallel gap reopening: EARLY WARNING - cash-market rates moved 0.4-1.0% above the official rate in late June 2026, NBU intervention burn jumped 53% m/m to $4.63bn in June, and its own corridor tolerates drift to ~47 UAH/USD; a cut-off of external aid would force a sharp devaluation

Reformer losing power: WATCH - no elections possible under martial law (extended to 2 Aug 2026), but US pressure for a vote is rising, the Energoatom scandal damaged Zelensky's reform credibility, and any ceasefire triggers elections within months with an untested outcome

Country risk blowing out: WATCH - bonds near post-restructuring highs are priced on peace hopes (2034 at ~62 cents); the June 2026 US deadline lapsed without a deal and bonds fell up to 1.9c on prior talk setbacks, so a definitive collapse of negotiations would reprice the whole curve

reformer.name: Hakainde Hichilema
reformer.role: President (UPND), with Finance Minister Situmbeko Musokotwane executing the program
reformer.nextElection: 2026-08-13 general election (presidential + parliamentary); Hichilema seeking second term
reformer.notes: Businessman-president elected Aug 2021 on an orthodox reform platform. Track record: completed 38-month IMF ECF (final review Jan 2026), restructured ~94% of external debt perimeter under G20 Common Framework, cut inflation from ~24% to 6.5%, ran a 3.1%-of-GDP primary surplus in 2025, rebuilt reserves to $6.4bn (4.4 months imports), improved Transparency Intl CPI rank 117th->92nd. Not Milei-grade shock therapy: orthodox IMF-anchored gradualism, and discipline is slipping pre-election (fuel VAT/excise suspension, ag subsidy overruns ~1.3% of GDP, civil-service wage hike). Political risk to program is LOW-MODERATE: opposition fragmented (14 approved candidates; main rival ex-president Lungu was disqualified by Constitutional Court and died June 2025); three smaller opposition parties endorsed Hichilema citing his reform record. Election is a binary event 5 weeks out; IMF successor-program talks deferred until after the vote. [Verifier: National Assembly dissolved 15 May 2026 ahead of the August 2026 general election - ministers (incl. Musokotwane) ceased to hold office and are campaigning; Hichilema remains President, so "reformer in power" stands but the election is the live risk.]

parallelFx.notes: Kwacha is free-floating and unified - parallel/black-market premium was a 1970s-1990s phenomenon (unified during early-1990s liberalization; academic sources on the premium are all historical). No current material parallel market found. Interbank/official rate fluctuated 18.03-18.39 ZMW/USD over July 1-5, 2026 (Wise/XE data); 18.86 on May 13, 2026 (Canary Compass). Kwacha APPRECIATED ~43% vs USD May 2025->May 2026 on copper, reserves build and carry inflows. Reversal risk: 28.9% of government securities held by non-residents (offshore carry trade) and fuel subsidies masking pass-through, per May 2026 independent macro note.

countryRisk.rating: Fitch B- stable (upgraded from Restricted Default, Nov 28, 2025); S&P CCC+/C stable (upgraded from SD, Nov 21, 2025); Moody's Caa2 positive (outlook to positive Apr 11, 2025)
countryRisk.notes: No verifiable current EMBI/USD-spread print found in fetched sources - set to null rather than guess. Verified price proxies: restructured 2053 step-up eurobond traded ~74 cents pre-buyback-offer, rising to ~79 cents in June 2026 (Mwebantu/CNBC Africa); Zambia offered ~78c and Morgan Stanley expected up to 85c for holdouts (Bloomberg, Jun 4, 2026); 97.85% of 2053 holders tendered into the $1.36bn buyback funded by a $600M AfDB loan. Local-currency 10Y yield fell to 16.92%, lowest since May 2017 (Trading Economics). Historical reference: weighted-average eurobond spread was 1,959 bps end-Oct 2023 (World Bank DSA) after peaking at 6,954 bps in Mar 2023 - spreads have compressed massively since restructuring. Two rating upgrades in eight days (Nov 2025) ended five years in default. Direction unambiguously improving.

ZMB.imfProgram: 38-month Extended Credit Facility (approved Aug 31, 2022, extended Sep 2025) COMPLETED: IMF Executive Board finished the sixth and final review on Jan 27, 2026, disbursing SDR 138.9M (~US$190M), total ~SDR 1,271.66M (~US$1.7bn). Zambia abandoned a planned 1-year extension (Bloomberg, Jan 7, 2026) and let the program expire; negotiations on a successor arrangement are expected to resume with the incoming government after the Aug 2026 elections. May 2026 Article-IV-style staff visit (Apr 30-May 13) praised inflation/reserves/debt progress but warned pre-election spending threatens fiscal gains - so Zambia is currently WITHOUT an active IMF anchor.

latestInflation.notes: YoY 6.5% in June vs 6.6% May, 6.8% April, 7.1% March, 11.2% Dec 2025 - inside the Bank of Zambia 6-8% target band, down from ~24% when Hichilema took office. M/m 0.1% in June vs 0.2% in May. Food 6.7%, non-food 6.0% YoY. BoZ cut the policy rate three times in 2026 to 13.25% (May 13, 2026; -75bp Feb, -25bp May). Caveats: IMF (May 2026) projects inflation RISING to ~8.5% by end-2026 on election spending; independent analysis notes suspended fuel VAT/excise is masking ~K6-7/liter of pump-price pressure.

ZMB.fiscalNote: 2025 outturn: overall fiscal deficit ~4.6% of GDP but PRIMARY SURPLUS of 3.1% of GDP (IMF). 2026 budget (K253.1bn, 27.4% of GDP, tabled by Musokotwane) targets a 2.1% overall deficit, but trajectory is deteriorating into the August election: IMF (May 2026) cut its 2026 primary-surplus projection to 1.1% of GDP from 3.8%, citing fuel VAT/excise suspension, civil-service wage rise, and ~1.3%-of-GDP agricultural subsidy overruns. Independent analysis (Canary Compass, May 13, 2026): Q1 deficit consumed ~40% of the annual target in two months, a K26.3bn supplementary budget was tabled Apr 30, and the overall 2026 deficit could reach ~4% of GDP - nearly double target. Public debt fell to ~87.6% of GDP in 2025 from 101.8% in 2024 (IMF); Fitch projects ~85% in 2026. Direction: consolidation achieved 2022-2025, now slipping in the election year.
developments: IMF Executive Board completed the sixth and FINAL review of Zambia's ECF on Jan 27, 2026, disbursing ~US$190M (total ~US$1.7bn); Zambia dropped its extension request and the program expired, leaving no IMF anchor until post-election successor talks [source: https://www.zambiainvest.com/economy/imf-extended-credit-facility-sixth-review/]

Fitch upgraded Zambia to B- (stable) from Restricted Default on Nov 28, 2025, and S&P to CCC+ (stable) from SD on Nov 21, 2025 - two upgrades in eight days ending five years in default, on ~94% completion of the external debt restructuring [source: https://www.investing.com/news/world-news/fitch-upgrades-zambia-to-b-with-stable-outlook-after-debt-restructuring-93CH-4382030]

June 2026: Zambia executed the 'world's first debt-for-energy swap' - a $1.36bn buyback of its 2053 restructured eurobond funded by a $600M AfDB loan, winning 97.85% bondholder participation after sweetening the offer; the bond rallied from ~74 to ~79 cents [source: https://www.cnbcafrica.com/2026/zambia-announces-early-results-of-1-36-billion-eurobond-tender-offer]

Inflation fell to 6.5% YoY in June 2026 (m/m 0.1%), a fifth straight monthly decline and inside the BoZ 6-8% target band; BoZ cut its policy rate a third time to 13.25% on May 13, 2026 [source: https://www.zamstats.gov.zm/annual-inflation-june-2025-june-2026/]

IMF staff visit (Apr 30-May 13, 2026) confirmed reserves at $6.4bn (4.4 months of imports) and restructuring at ~94% of perimeter, but warned pre-election spending, fuel-tax suspension and ag-subsidy overruns will slash the 2026 primary surplus to ~1.1% of GDP from the 3.8% previously programmed [source: https://www.mwebantu.com/imf-says-zambia-has-made-impressive-progress-on-inflation-reserves-and-debt-restructuring-but-faces-fiscal-pressure-before-elections/amp/]

Q1 2026 real GDP grew 7.7% YoY (vs 4.5% a year earlier), led by agriculture +21.4% after the drought recovery; ZamStats release of June 25, 2026 [source: https://www.indexbox.io/blog/zambia-inflation-eases-to-65-in-june-2026-as-gdp-grows-77-in-q1/]

Kwacha appreciated ~43% vs USD between May 2025 and May 2026 and traded ~18.0-18.4/USD in early July 2026; local 10Y yield hit 16.92%, lowest since May 2017, on a foreign carry-trade bid [source: https://wise.com/gb/currency-converter/usd-to-zmw-rate/history]

Election Aug 13, 2026: Hichilema launched his re-election campaign as strong favorite against a fragmented field of 14 approved candidates; ex-president Lungu was disqualified by the Constitutional Court and died in June 2025; three opposition parties endorsed Hichilema citing his economic record [source: https://africacenter.org/spotlight/en-elections-2026/zambia/]
killCriteriaWatch: FISCAL SLIPPAGE (closest to tripping): primary surplus projected to collapse from 3.8% to 1.1% of GDP in 2026 (IMF, May 2026); Q1 deficit consumed ~40% of the annual target in two months, a K26.3bn supplementary budget was tabled, fuel VAT/excise suspended, ag subsidies overran by ~1.3% of GDP, and the April domestic bond auction was only 43% subscribed - overall deficit could hit ~4% of GDP vs 2.1% target. Budget has NOT flipped to primary deficit yet, but the trend is wrong.

IMF ANCHOR REMOVED: ECF expired Jan 2026 with no successor until after the Aug 13 election - fiscal discipline is currently unenforced ('fiscal discipline collapsed three months post-programme' per independent May 2026 note). Watch whether a new IMF arrangement is negotiated in H2 2026.

REFORMER LOSING POWER: binary event Aug 13, 2026. Risk looks low (fragmented 14-candidate opposition, Lungu dead/disqualified, cross-party endorsements of Hichilema), but a surprise loss or disputed result would gut the thesis.

HOT-MONEY REVERSAL RISK (early warning, not tripped): kwacha strength rests partly on a carry trade - non-residents hold 28.9% of government securities and supplied ~76% of Q1 net issuance; a post-election fiscal blowout or global risk-off could reverse the FX gain. No parallel-market gap exists to reopen; country risk is compressing, not blowing out.
